event.title: <show-title>Project Hail Mary</show-title> <event-type-title>Book Club Screening</event-type-title>
event.headline: Free sticker and bookmark for book readers
event.description:
Andy Weir fans, rejoice! At this special screening for book readers, you’ll get a free bookmark passport with a custom PROJECT HAIL MARY sticker to track your novel-to-movie journey. Whether you got your first sticker at WUTHERING HEIGHTS or are making your maiden Book Club voyage, join us and find out if the Rocky in your head matches the one depicted on screen.

Select locations will also feature a Book Fair in the bar after the movie, with local booksellers offering their wares while you sip a draft beer or cocktail:

  • Cedars
  • DTLA
  • Littleton
  • Lower Manhattan
  • Raleigh
  • South Lamar

eventType.title: Book Club Screening
eventType.headline: Collect custom stickers as you enjoy your favorite movie adaptations
eventType.description: Celebrate your favorite book-turned-movie with the Alamo Drafthouse Movie Book Club. Get your passport at the show, then fill it with custom-themed stickers at each Book Club screening. Join us in the bar after the film to connect with local bookstores, libraries, and other book lovers. Keep reading and keep watching!

show.title: Longlegs
show.headline: A disturbing case hits a little close to home for a rookie FBI agent

event.title: <show-title>Longlegs</show-title> <event-type-title>Free Victory Screening</event-type-title>
event.headline: Nicolas Cage at his weirdest
event.description:
From horror maestro Osgood Perkins (THE MONKEY) comes a case of creeping terror with a sinister performance from the one, the only, Nicolas Cage. After being assigned an unresolved serial killer case, rookie FBI agent Lee Harker (Maika Monroe, IT FOLLOWS) must follow cryptic, occult clues to bring an end to the infamous killer’s spree. The deeper she goes, though, the more personal the case becomes.

LONGLEGS is a bit like an ominous riddle that you can't help but puzzle over, even as you realize the answer will only scare the pants off you. It’s a deliciously creepy thriller with an unhinged villain performance from Nicolas Cage (as if he gives any other kind). Reserve your spot now by purchasing a fully refundable food and beverage voucher redeemable for snacks and non-alcoholic drinks at the show.

imageAsset.title: Raccoon City Cinema Menu Web Deluxe Showpage 1920x1080

image.altText: Raccoon City Cinema Menu Web Deluxe Showpage 1920x1080

contentBlocks.paragraphs: Before CAPCOM’s Resident Evil Requiem hits PlayStation® 5, catch four unsettling horror classics on the big screen: THE THING (1982), THE RING, THE CABIN IN THE WOODS, and LONGLEGS. Together, these films capture what Resident Evil has always done best: making you feel like the worst decisions were made long before you arrived, and now all that’s left is survival. These screenings will also feature an exclusive menu inspired by the Resident Evil series, including the “Bingo!” Fried Chicken Plate and the T-Virus Cocktail. Reserve your spot now by purchasing a fully refundable food and beverage voucher redeemable for snacks and non-alcoholic drinks at the show.

show.headline: He doesn't care if you're naughty or nice

event.title: <show-title>Bad Santa</show-title> <event-type-title>Free Victory Screening</event-type-title>
event.headline: Programmed by Gorillaz, ahead of their album The Mountain
event.description:
To celebrate the release of The Mountain on Feb. 27, the band Gorillaz is taking over Alamo Drafthouse with a full month of Guest Selects screenings, each programmed by a different member of the world’s most animated band.

On March 12, Murdoc selects BAD SANTA, Terry Zwigoff’s deeply inappropriate holiday classic about a department store Santa who drinks like a fish and steals like a raccoon. The film will be preceded by a short film, Gorillaz Presents The Mountain, The Moon Cave and The Sad God.

event.title: <show-title>Perfect Days</show-title> <event-type-title>Free Victory Screening</event-type-title>
event.headline: Programmed by Gorillaz, ahead of their album The Mountain
event.description:
To celebrate the release of The Mountain on Feb. 27, the band Gorillaz is taking over Alamo Drafthouse with a full month of Guest Selects screenings, each programmed by a different member of the world’s most animated band.

On Thursday, February 26th, Alamo Drafthouse will host an exclusive album release event: Gorillaz Presents The Mountain, The Moon Cave and The Sad God, plus a short documentary film – an Alamo-only 20-minute special video presentation – followed by a screening of Wim Wenders’ 2023 film PERFECT DAYS. Attendees of this one-night-only album release event will receive a limited-edition keepsake, available exclusively at this screening.

show.title: The Ultimate Willy Wonka Party
show.headline: Scrumdiddlyumptious

event.title: <show-title>The Ultimate Willy Wonka Party</show-title><event-type-title> with Live Q&A</event-type-title>
event.headline: See Willy Wonka with Mike Teevee in person + chocolate from Trugalug!
event.description:
Come with me, and you’ll be in a wooorld of pure imagination...and you’ll also be in a theater with one of the original kids from WILLY WONKA & THE CHOCOLATE FACTORY!

We’ll have a full Q&A after the screening, so you can ask Paris Themmen (Mike TeeVee) what it was like to be so tiny. Plus, you’ll get a tasty gift box of chocolate from our new neighbors at Trugalug!

Whether you’re a kid or just a kid at heart, this candy-colored movie is a delightful confection that you’ll enjoy again and again. And for this screening, we’re pulling out all the stops with a glass of delicious hot chocolate from the chocolate river, golden goose feathers, and your very own golden ticket.

We’d hate to ruin it all for you, so sign the contract on the bottom line and enter the magical world of Willy Wonka…

eventType.title: Live Q&A
eventType.headline: Exclusive conversations with actors and filmmakers
eventType.description: Got a question for the director? Wanna meet the cast? At our special Live Q&A events, we follow the screening with an in-person discussion.

show.title: Jurassic Park
show.headline: Spielberg's rampaging dinosaur masterpiece proves life finds a way

event.title: <show-title>Jurassic Park</show-title> <event-type-title>Movie Party</event-type-title>
event.headline: Life finds a way to party
event.description:
Hold onto your butts, it’s the epic interactive screening 65 million years in the making!

John Hammond was a man with a simple dream of satisfying his lawyers and insurance agents so that he could open a park where humans would once again be free to walk the Earth with massive creatures that enjoy killing them. From that dream, a new legend was hatched (under BD Wong’s close observation, of course) – our JURASSIC PARK Movie Party. So get in your self-driving electric Jeep and pay attention to the rippling water in that plastic cup as you join us for a cinematic experience unlike any other!

We spared no expense for Steven Spielberg’s all-time classic. Check the boxes in the back of your Jeeps (aka your table) for glow sticks, hand soap, a car magnet, a ladle, dino poop, and everything else you need for a fun-filled time with some two-story-tall genetic monstrosities. After all, what could possibly go wrong?

eventType.title: Movie Party
eventType.headline: Celebrate your fave movies with fun, interactive props, themed drinks, and more
eventType.description: Spanning all genres, Alamo Drafthouse Movie Parties are an immersive, interactive experience for cinema fans featuring fun props, themed drinks, and more.

titleCardImage.altText: Movie Party TC

show.title: Jurassic Park III
show.headline: Dr. Grant returns to Isla Sorna to face the deadliest dinosaurs yet

event.title: <show-title>Jurassic Park III</show-title> <event-type-title>Movie Party</event-type-title>
event.headline: It's not just a walk in the park at our interactive movie party
event.description:
In need of funds for research, Dr. Alan Grant accepts a large sum of money to accompany Paul and Amanda Kirby on an aerial tour of the infamous Isla Sorna. It isn't long before all hell breaks loose and the stranded wayfarers must fight for survival as a host of new – and even more deadly – dinosaurs try to make snacks of them.

Your ticket includes the following props:

  • Dino Parachute – Try to save yourself from being pteranodon food.
  • T. Rex Pee Air Freshener – You don't want to know how we got this.
  • "Alan!" Sticker – If you had to return to a park full of dinosaurs, you'd also be having fever dreams.

show.title: The Lost World: Jurassic Park
show.headline: Something has survived

event.title: <show-title>The Lost World: Jurassic Park</show-title> <event-type-title>Movie Party</event-type-title>
event.headline: Something has survived at our interactive movie party
event.description:
Four years after JURASSIC PARK became a cultural phenomenon, Steven Spielberg and Jeff Goldblum would team up again to adapt Michael Crichton's follow-up THE LOST WORLD into a movie. Featuring a setup not far from that of the original – silly humans put dinosaurs on another island, sillier humans go to said island to study them, more silly humans show up to fight about it – THE LOST WORLD: JURASSIC PARK is a darker, more visceral outing that still manages to capture the wonder of the first film. And, yes, the effects still hold up.

Your ticket includes the following props:

  • Grow your own dino kit – They grow up so fast.
  • Gum – Perfect for when you need something pliable as a bandage.
  • Angry Mommy sticker – Steal a baby dinosaur, get a very angry mommy.
  • Headband – The long grass can be totally killer.

show.title: The Mummy Returns: 25th Anniversary
show.headline: Do you smell what the Scorpion King is cooking?

event.title: <show-title>The Mummy Returns: 25th Anniversary</show-title> <event-type-title>Movie Party</event-type-title>
event.headline: Do you smell what the Scorpion King is cooking?
event.description:
Director Stephen Sommers’ THE MUMMY RETURNS is not only the action-packed follow-up to his beloved 1999 original, but it also marks the big screen debut of one Dwayne Douglas “The Rock” Johnson. Nine years after condemning Imhotep (Arnold Vosloo) back to the underworld, Rick (Brendan Fraser) and Evelyn (Rachel Weisz) have settled down, had a quick-witted kid, and are living their best adventurer lives. Unfortunately for them, a reincarnated version of Anck-su-namun (Patricia Velasquez) and a few more Imhotep-worshiping knuckleheads are hellbent on bringing the mummy back so that he can claim the unstoppable power of an ancient warlord, the Scorpion King (Johnson). Cue the desert-trotting adventure.

While not as revered as its predecessor, THE MUMMY RETURNS is still a grand journey with big thrills and some laugh-out-loud moments. Fraser and Weisz bring charm in a big way, and it’s fun to see The Rock in a movie before he became one of the biggest stars in Hollywood – even if he’s covered in CGI that isn’t, like, the greatest ever. That’s all part of the charm though. Plus, Anubis’ army of jackal-headed warriors is still rad as hell.

Your ticket includes the following props:

  • Black snake – The perfect weapon to throw at your enemies.
  • Scorpion bracelet – Don't worry, you can wear this for more than seven days with no issues.
  • Medjai sticker – This is a symbol of your sacred duty.
  • Combat mask – Wearing this may help you unlock memories of your past life.

show.title: On Cinema at the Cinema Special
show.headline: We've got the Oscar Fever. Hope you got it too.

show.title: Dolly
show.headline: Mommy knows best.

event.title: <show-title>Dolly</show-title> <event-type-title>in 35mm</event-type-title>
event.headline: The latest in backwoods horror.
event.description:
Chase and Macy drop off Chase’s daughter, Evy, and then hike to a nearby overlook where he plans to propose. Those plans are soon forgotten when they discover a weird ‘art installation’ of dirty, broken dolls.

Unfortunately the dolls are actually more of an altar to the victims of Dolly—a violent, hulking, child-like person in a tattered dress and a porcelain doll mask—who arrives to bury the decapitated corpse of their latest plaything. While they have little interest in Chase outside of violence, Macy catches their eye and is taken home to become their newest doll.

With the help of a voice in the walls, Macy quickly realizes she must navigate her captor’s delusions and family trauma to have any hope of surviving the nightmare and the filthy house she finds herself in. Of course that means playing her role of a doll—including diaper changes and feedings—until she can find the right moment to make her move.

Nothing is ever quite as it seems, except of course for the absolutely goopy practical effects in this lo-fi throwback to the isolated murder families of THE HILLS HAVE EYES and the original TEXAS CHAIN SAW MASSACRE. Director Rod Blackhurst combines his clear inspiration with a uniquely demented point of view, building on his previous work, including HERE ALONE and writing NIGHT SWIM. Fabianne Therese gives a strong final girl performance as Macy, squaring off beautifully to transmasc wrestler Max the Impaler’s unspeaking Dolly, both of whom are joined by pitch-perfect performances from Seann William Scott and Ethan Suplee. (KEV DOOLEY)

35mm print courtesy of Independent Film Company and Shudder

show.title: The Red Spectacles
show.headline: Mamoru Oshii's live action stunner.

event.title: <show-title>The Red Spectacles</show-title>
event.headline: Mamoru Oshii's anti-fascist live action stunner.
event.description:
Presented in Japanese with English subtitles

THE RED SPECTACLES is a dystopian futuristic comedy, equal parts Godard’s ALPHAVILLE and Chris Marker’s LA JETEE. The first film in Oshii’s Kerberos Saga, the film follows Todome (Shigeru Chiba), a former police detective who flees Tokyo after his elite police unit is shut down due to its fanatical approach to crime fighting. After three years on the run, Todome returns to Tokyo—unrecognizable due to decay and neglect—hoping to reunite with the two trusted colleagues he had to leave behind. (Wexner Center for the Arts)

show.title: Mystery Voyage

show.title: Daisies
show.headline: Two teenage girls embark on a series of destructive pranks

event.title: <show-title>Daisies</show-title>
event.headline: Věra Chytilová’s stunning surrealist “necrologue” of femininity in revolt
event.description:
“We’ll have to think of a worse kind of life.”

It’s difficult to feel sane and stable in our current era, with all that’s happening or not happening; it’s easy to feel as though the world has become a spoiled and unyielding place devoid of empathy and grace. This isn’t new; though the world continues to throw inventively bleak and troubling bullshit our way, filmmakers have been using the medium to break down and critique societal failures since the form's invention. In the 1960s, Czechoslovakian cinema was in the midst of a “new wave” boom, seeing the rise of new voices like Miloš Forman, Jaromil Jireš', and Věra Chytilová creating films that challenged and satirized life under the communist regime. Chytilova’s landmark film DAISIES uses surrealism and dark, absurdist humor to question the indulgences and indifferences of the privileged members of society during an era of unrest, war, and lack.

What’s the film about? Great question! Like all good art, the film asks far more questions than it answers. Two young girls, both named Marie, find themselves bored and despondent in the face of the world’s “spoiled” nature, so they decide to become just as “spoiled” as the world. The two proto-Jokers embark on a series of disruptions, pranks, and grotesque feasts while rejecting the patriarchy and seemingly losing their minds. The relationship between the Marie’s blends elements of queerness, anarchy, radical indulgence, and destruction into a pureed pulp of insanity that’s up for interpretation. Heralded as an arthouse feminist masterpiece, DAISIES was initially banned for its subversive “attacks on establishment values.” The film is now considered one of the greatest avant-garde art films ever made. And now you get to say you’ve seen it on the big screen and really be a cinephile. Not that that matters. Is that something that matters? “Do you really think it matters?”

titleCardImage.altText: Event Category Web Hero Special Event 1920x1080

show.title: Melancholia
show.headline: A mysterious new planet threatens to collide with Earth

event.title: <show-title>Melancholia</show-title>
event.headline: A mysterious new planet threatens to collide with Earth
event.description:
On what should be the happiest day of her life, Justine (Kirsten Dunst) is grappling with severe depression. Her sister, Claire (Charlotte Gainsbourg), is trying her best to help her sister through the wedding, but her help comes across as controlling because her husband is paying for the whole extravaganza and has a rigid schedule. However, everything begins to unravel as a mysterious blue planet called Melancholia hurls towards the Earth.

Director Lars Von Trier developed MELANCHOLIA during a therapy session, when his therapist noted that depressed people tend to act calm during high-pressure events because they already anticipate the worst. Possibly his most accessible yet personal film in his oeuvre, MELANCHOLIA is a deeply beautiful examination of the human psyche under pressure.

titleCardImage.altText: Event Category Web Hero Special Event 1920x1080

show.title: Maximum Overdrive
show.headline: Maximum Terror. Maximum King.

event.title: <show-title>Maximum Overdrive</show-title>
event.headline: Stephen King's manic big-rig thrillride.
event.description:
If there’s one thing that keeps Stephen King fans coming back again and again, it’s that the man knows how to have fun. Mixed into his catalog of highbrow horror and tender dramas are pulpy, over-the-top beach reads full of juicy characters and bloody mayhem. That’s where MAXIMUM OVERDRIVE fits in.

Written and directed by King himself, the film centers around the Dixie Boy, a mom 'n' pop truck stop under siege after a bizarro cosmic incident infects every Earthly machine from lawnmowers to giant 18-wheelers with a thirst for murder. High-octane havoc ensues.

With a great cast led by Emilio Estevez, a slammin’ soundtrack by none other than AC/DC, and loads of gore and goofball humor, MAXIMUM OVERDRIVE is the ultimate in Stephen King excess.

show.title: Saw II
show.headline: I want to play a game . . . again.

event.title: <show-title>Saw II</show-title>
event.headline: I want to play a game in 35mm . . . again
event.description:
If Michael Bay directed a SAW movie, it would feel a lot like SAW II. And that's the highest compliment we can pay to this hyperactive, near-perfect snapshot of 2000s mayhem. Picking up shortly after the events of SAW, this first sequel finds malicious serial killer Jigsaw commandeering his own trap-filled murderhouse to target a group of ex-convicts—and also Donnie "brother of Mark" Wahlberg. With production design that resembles a stoned evening at Universal’s Halloween Horror Nights and enough grisly carnage to fill three movies, SAW II is campy, outrageous, and deliciously gross.

Screening an archival 35mm print

show.title: Thirst
show.headline: Lusting after sinful pleasures

event.title: <show-title>Thirst</show-title>
event.headline: Park Chan-wook’s vampire "romance."
event.description:
Screening in Korean with English subtitles.

A box office smash in Korea and the winner of the Prix du Jury at the 2009 Cannes Film Festival, THIRST is a vampire "romance" that could only escape from the mind of director Park Chan-wook (OLDBOY). Sang-hyun (Song Kang-ho, PARASITE) is a priest who inadvertently becomes a vampire. Struggling between morality and bloodlust, Sang-hyun meets Tae-ju (Kim Ok-vin) and the couple embark on a grim path of sex, murder, and self-discovery. Psychedelic, violent, and heartwrenching, THIRST is a masterful horror film from Korea’s finest purveyor of sublime agony.

DCP courtesy of Universal Pictures

show.title: Crumb
show.headline: A portrait of an underground artist.

event.title: <show-title>Crumb</show-title>
event.headline: Terry Zwigoff's portrait of an underground artist.
event.description:
David Lynch presents one of the most critically acclaimed films ever made. A hilarious and mysterious journey through artistic genius and sexual obsession, CRUMB is a wild ride through the mind of Robert Crumb; creator of Zap Comix, Mr. Natural and Fritz the Cat. CRUMB enters a territory as spooky as it is fascinating... a portrait of the artist as misanthrope, as bad-boy visionary, as a joker and sex maniac and, finally, as hero. One of those rare film experiences that has the giddy effect of being a nightmare and a party at the same time. (Sony Pictures Classics)

4k DCP courtesy of Sony Pictures Classics

event.headline: A gravedigger goes to great lengths to resurrect her dead lover
event.description: A lonely Gravedigger who stinks of corpses finally meets her dream man, but their whirlwind affair is cut short when he tragically drowns at sea. Grief-stricken, she goes to morbid lengths to resurrect him through madcap scientific experiments, resulting in grave consequences and unlikely love.

show.title: Fear (1996)
show.headline: Together forever. Or else.

event.title: <show-title>Fear (1996)</show-title>
event.headline: Together 4eva... or else.
event.description:
Teenaged Nicole (Reese Witherspoon), newly installed in her father’s fancy remote Seattle home, introduces an unsavory element when she falls for David (Mark Wahlberg), a polite boy with an unknown background and sketchy friends. Dad (William Petersen) is immediately on guard, so when David’s aggressive, obsessive tendencies bring a threat to the family home, his high tech security and surveillance, as well as his dad-bod, is put to the test.

A worthwhile thriller that has grown a cult following in the twenty years since its release, FEAR captures the anxiety of an upper middle class father who can’t control the choices of his daughter. Show up for evil Wahlberg, and be treated to an unforgettable roller coaster orgasm scene, and the most wild love shrine you’ll see in a movie. (Cristina Cacioppo)

show.title: Dolly
show.headline: Mommy knows best.

event.title: <show-title>Dolly</show-title> <event-type-title>Early Access</event-type-title>
event.headline: A doll-like person abducts a woman and plans to raise her as their child
event.description:
Chase and Macy drop off Chase’s daughter, Evy, and then hike to a nearby overlook where he plans to propose. Those plans are soon forgotten when they discover a weird "art installation" of dirty, broken dolls.

Unfortunately, the dolls are actually more of an altar to the victims of Dolly – a violent, hulking, child-like person in a tattered dress and a porcelain doll mask – who arrives to bury the decapitated corpse of their latest plaything. While they have little interest in Chase outside of violence, Macy catches their eye and is taken home to become their newest doll.

With the help of a voice in the walls, Macy quickly realizes she must navigate her captor’s delusions and family trauma to have any hope of surviving the nightmare and the filthy house she finds herself in. Of course, that means playing her role of a doll – including diaper changes and feedings – until she can find the right moment to make her move.

Nothing is ever quite as it seems, except of course for the absolutely goopy practical effects in this lo-fi throwback to the isolated murder families of THE HILLS HAVE EYES and the original TEXAS CHAIN SAW MASSACRE. Director Rod Blackhurst combines his clear inspiration with a uniquely demented point of view, building on his previous work, including HERE ALONE and writing NIGHT SWIM. Fabianne Therese gives a strong final girl performance as Macy, squaring off beautifully to transmasc wrestler Max the Impaler’s unspeaking Dolly, both of whom are joined by pitch-perfect performances from Seann William Scott and Ethan Suplee. (Kev Dooley)

show.title: Twin Peaks: The Return
show.headline: Twenty-five years after the murder of Laura Palmer, the story continues

event.title: <show-title>Twin Peaks: The Return</show-title> <event-type-title>Parts 10-12</event-type-title>
event.description:
Part 10 | 60 mins.
The search for Cooper continues; more pieces of the puzzle come together. Richard Horne's violent behavior escalates, and Dougie's family faces danger.

Part 11 | 60 mins.
Events in Twin Peaks take a dark turn; Dougie's fate hangs in the balance. The FBI moves closer to finding Cooper, and a child is hit by a car.

Part 12 | 60 mins.
The investigation leads to unexpected places; familiar faces return. Audrey Horne makes a troubling appearance, and Sarah Palmer's behavior is erratic.

event.description:
Part 13 | 60 mins.
Cooper's return seems imminent; old secrets are unearthed. Dougie wins a jackpot, and the Mitchum brothers' storyline unfolds.

Part 14 | 60 mins.
Cooper's allies make progress; the true nature of the Black Lodge is explored. Gordon Cole has a vision, and Andy visits the White Lodge.

Part 15 | 60 mins.
Cooper's journey reaches a critical point; the past and present collide. Big Ed and Norma's romance blossoms, and Steven and Gersten's story takes a dark turn.

Part 16 | 60 mins.
Cooper regains his identity; a showdown looms. Diane's true nature is revealed, and Dougie says goodbye to his family.

Part 17 | 60 mins.
Cooper confronts the ultimate evil; the truth about Laura Palmer is revealed. The final showdown with Mr. C occurs, and Judy's role is hinted at.

Part 18 | 60 mins.
Cooper's journey comes to a surreal and ambiguous conclusion. Cooper and Diane cross into another reality, leaving their fates uncertain.

event.title: <show-title>Twin Peaks: The Return</show-title> <event-type-title>Parts 7-9</event-type-title>
event.description:
Part 7 | 60 mins.
The investigation in Twin Peaks intensifies. Cooper/Dougie's behavior raises suspicion. Major Briggs' body is found, and Diane is brought into the investigation.

Part 8 | 60 mins.
A surreal journey into the origins of evil; the birth of BOB is revealed. The episode explores the atomic bomb test and its supernatural consequences.

Part 9 | 60 mins.
Cooper's friends work to bring him back; the mystery deepens. The search for Dougie's origins continues, and the FBI makes a breakthrough.

marketStrings.value: The personal closed captioning device displays feature film dialogue and other audio in the form of English-language text for individuals who are deaf or hard of hearing.

The descriptive audio headset provides individuals who are blind or have low vision with an English-language narration of the details of the feature presentation that can't be understood by the soundtrack alone.

Upon request at the box office, we provide assistive listening headsets with amplified audio for those individuals who are hard of hearing. For guests who prefer to use their own hearing aids, neck loops, or cochlear implants, our assistive listening headsets have a 3.5mm headphone jack to connect to those devices.

In order to ensure the highest quality experience for all guests, you may not enter a movie after it has started. We’d love to see you up to 30 minutes early. Running late? No worries, we will happily exchange your ticket for a different showtime or a raincheck to a future show.

sessionAttributes.name: Baby Day Show
sessionAttributes.description: For our baby day shows, we keep the lights up a bit, and the sound down a bit, so your little one never has to know they even went to the movies!

sessionAttributes.name: Kid Friendly
sessionAttributes.description: Grab the fam and let the fun begin! Our Kid Friendly screenings include pre-show activity sheets and crayons, a relaxed noise policy, and kids menus. Ages 3+ welcome with a parent or guardian.
